sections.paragraph:
, in pause פֶּ֑רִי, with suff. פִּרְיִי, פִּרְיֹו; but פֶּרְיְךָ, פֶּרְיְכֶם Hos 14:9 Eze 36:8 and פְּרִיהֶם, פְּרִיהֶן Amo 9:11 Jer 29:28, m. (from the root פָּרָה).

(1) fruit, whether of the earth and field (corn), Gen 4:3 Isa 4:2 Psa 72:16 107:34 , or of a tree, Gen 1:12, 29 whence עֵץ פְּרִי fruit-bearing tree, Gen 1:11. Metaph. used of the result of labour or endeavour, the image often being preserved. Isa 3:10, “ye shall eat the fruit of your hands;” ye shall experience the results; Pro 1:31 Jer 6:19 17:10 Psa 104:13, “the earth is satisfied with the fruit of thy works;” i.e. it is watered with rain, which is the fruit of the sky and clouds. Pro 31:16, פְּרִי כַפַּיִם “the fruit of hands;” i.e. gain. Isa 10:12, פְּרִי־גֹדֶל לֵבָב “the fruit of pride;” used of boasting.

(2) offspring, Lam 2:20 with the addition of בֶּטֶן Gen 30:2 Deu 7:13 28:4 .
